question:
Will Finland vs. Andorra end in a draw?

Description: In the upcoming game, scheduled for November 17, 2025,
If the game ends in a draw, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve to "Yes".

Resolution source: https://www.fifa.com

evidences.content: In this qualifying campaign, they lost to England by scorelines of 1-0 and 2-0, and yet again were beaten 1-0 by Albania in their previous outing. They conceded an average of two goals per game, but their defensive resilience is commendable, which is what coach Koldo Alvarez will be depending on in this encounter. [...] The former Norwich City man goes out as the national team’s all-time highest goalscorer, which is a huge loss for the home side. Daniel Hakans will not be available for selection after undergoing surgery, which is the primary injury concern for coach Friis. The visitors have a full-strength squad for the friendly match, giving Alvarez the ability to rotate his team. evidences.content: c) No additional substitutes may be added to the list of players on the start list. d) The teams can make only the number of substitutions to which they were still entitled when the match was abandoned. e) Players sent off during the abandoned match cannot be replaced. f) Any disciplinary measures imposed before the match was abandoned will be dealt with in accordance with the FIFA Disciplinary Code. g) The kick-off time, date and location shall be decided by FIFA. 5.8 If a participating club withdraws and/or is disqualified prior to the end of the group stage, the results of all of its matches will be declared null and void. [...] ARTICLE 44: MATTERS NOT PROVIDED FOR Any matters not provided for in these Regulations and any cases of force majeure shall be decided by FIFA. All decisions shall be final and binding and not subject to appeal. ARTICLE 45: LANGUAGES In the case of any discrepancy in the interpretation of the English, French or Spanish texts of these Regulations, or any other language versions thereof, the English text shall be authoritative. evidences.content: The 2025 edition contains the recent changes and amendments to the most relevant FIFA regulations and rules, including the up-to-date versions of the FIFA Governance Regulations, the FIFA Disciplinary Code, the FIFA Regulations on the Status and Transfer of Players and the FIFA Clearing House Regulations, amongst others. The FIFA Legal Handbook is available in English, Spanish and French on legal.fifa.com. 🤔 Have you got a spare minute? Help us make Inside FIFA articles better for you and millions of active users. Give my feedback [...] FIFA ### FIFA publishes 2025 edition of Legal Handbook Publication includes latest regulations, legal guides, documents and circulars Providing valuable support to the international football community Full document available on legal.fifa.com FIFA has today published the 2025 edition of its Legal Handbook, which again features a unique overview of the latest regulations, legal guides, documents and circulars issued by world football’s governing body, thus providing valuable support to the international football community.

evidences.title: The IFAB introduces further measures to improve match ...
evidences.content: - Law 10 (Determining the Outcome of a Match) and Law 14 (The Penalty Kick): Incorporation of the clarification contained in circular 31 (June 2025) regarding an accidental “double touch” by the penalty taker. - Law 12 (Fouls and Misconduct): Where the referee applies advantage for an offence for (denying an obvious goalscoring opportunity and a goal is scored), the offending player will not be cautioned, as the offence did not prevent a goal. [...] - Law 3 (The Players): The number of permitted substitutes in senior ‘A’ international friendly matches is increased to eight; both teams may agree to a further increase up to a maximum of eleven. - Law 4 (The Player’s Equipment): Non-dangerous items will be permitted if safely and securely covered. - Law 5 (The Referee): Referee body cameras (chest- or head-mounted) may be used as a competition option, with the competition providing the cameras and controlling the use of the footage. - Law 8 (The Start and Restart of Play): Clarification that the ball is dropped for the team that would have had or retained possession had play not been stopped. [...] FIFA ###### Media Release Fédération Internationale de Football Association FIFA Str...

evidences.title: Eight Minnesota United Players Called up for International Duty | MNUFC2
evidences.content: Robin Lod will rejoin the Finland national team as they continue their 2026 FIFA World Cup Qualifying campaign in UEFA Group G. The Huuhkajat are set to host Malta on Nov. 14 at the Helsinki Olympic Stadium before traveling to face Andorra on Nov. 17. Finland currently competes in Group G alongside the Netherlands, Poland, Lithuania, Malta, and Andorra. Lod, one of Finland’s most experienced players, has earned more than 80 caps since making his international debut in 2015 and remains a vital figure in Finland’s midfield as the team pushes for its first-ever World Cup appearance. evidences.title: xGaura — Free Football Stats, xG Predictions & Betting Tools
evidences.content: xGaura's mathematical predictions use the Poisson distribution model, a well-established statistical method used widely in sports analytics. The model takes each team's average goals scored and conceded over their most recent 38 matches, adjusts for home advantage and Elo rating strength, then calculates the probability of every possible scoreline. From this, we derive: 1X2 probabilities — the percentage chance of a home win, draw or away win. Over/under probabilities — the likelihood of the match producing more or fewer than 2.5 goals. BTTS probability — the chance that both teams score at least once. Most likely scorelines — the four highest-probability exact scores for each fixture. [...] Champions League Europa League Relegation [...] Champions League Europa League Relegation

evidences.content: This section contains these legal documents in the context of the activities of the FIFA Legal & Compliance Division, all downloadable in PDF format. FIFA, through its Congress, the FIFA Council or the Secretary General, passes various types of legal documents that have an impact on the day-to-day activities of football all over the world.

evidences.title: Competition - Simple English Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia
evidences.content: Wikipedia # Competition A competition or contest is an event where people get together to see who is the best at something. Someone who takes part in a competition ("enters for a competition") is called a competitor. The competitors compete against one another. The person who wins may get a prize. The prize might be a trophy or money. Competitions can be for lots of different things, for example, sport, writing, music, dancing, science, or chess. Some competitions need a referee. The referee will make sure that the game is played fairly and that the competitors obey the rules. In events like music competitions there will often be an adjudicator who will decide which person they think is the best. ## Contents ## Forms The adjective of 'competition' is competitive. [...] ## Forms The adjective of 'competition' is competitive. ## Examples of competitions There are a lot of different kinds of competitions, for example: or see the gallery of images below: ## Related pages ## References #### Languages Wikipedia Wikimedia Foundation Powered by MediaWiki
